judgment.summary: Extremely high growth metrics are offset by significant debt and negative free cash flow.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 34.5%; next earnings date is 2026-08-04.
positives: Revenue growth 122.7% YoY and Earnings growth 326.0% YoY indicate explosive top and bottom line expansion.
Quarterly earnings growth of 344.4% shows strong recent performance.
Forward P/E of 10.37x is attractive relative to current growth rates.
Analyst mean target of $37.13 suggests upside from the current price.
Current ratio of 2.65 and quick ratio of 1.20 indicate good short-term liquidity.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 120.80 and Total debt of $9.15B against $1.31B cash represent significant leverage.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $-7.45B is a major concern, indicating the company is burning cash.
Gross margin of 8.4% and Profit margin of 3.7% are relatively thin given the growth.
Analyst consensus is 'hold', not a strong buy signal.

news.paragraph: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) is facing some unrelated headline noise with Taiwan seeking to detain individuals in a crackdown on smuggling, which could indirectly impact supply chains. Meanwhile, Mizuho maintained a neutral rating on the stock, suggesting a balanced view from at least one analyst. The upcoming earnings report on August 4th will be a key catalyst to watch for any directional clarity.

headlines.title: Taiwan Seeks to Detain Three in Nvidia Smuggling Crackdown
Mizuho Maintains a Neutral Rating on Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI)
